volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 871 - Section 1044)

sections.label: Section 871
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ὡς ἄρʼ ἡμὶν οὐ σμικρῶν κακῶν
sections.translation: Ah , my daughters , great , indeed , are the sorrows for us that began with the gift sent to Heracles !

sections.label: Section 873
sections.original: τί δʼ , ὦ γεραιά , καινοποιηθὲν λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Old woman , what new trouble have you to tell ?

sections.label: Section 874
sections.translation: Deianeira has departed on the last

sections.label: Section 875
sections.translation: of all her journeys , departed without stirring a foot .

sections.label: Section 876a
sections.translation: Surely you do not mean she is dead ?

sections.label: Section 877a
sections.translation: Dead , the poor woman ?

sections.label: Section 878
sections.original: τάλαινʼ ὀλεθρία · τίνι τρόπῳ θανεῖν σφε φής ;
sections.translation: Poor , lost one ! Tell me , what was the manner of her death ?

sections.label: Section 880
sections.translation: woman , with what end did she meet ?

sections.label: Section 885
sections.translation: this death upon death , accomplishing it all on her own ?

sections.label: Section 886
sections.translation: By a stroke of the sword which gives cause for mourning .

sections.label: Section 887
sections.original: ἐπεῖδες , ὦ ματαία , τάνδε τὴν ὕβριν ;
sections.translation: Did you witness that violent deed , poor helpless one ?

sections.label: Section 888
sections.translation: I did , yes , since I was standing close by .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.translation: What was it ? How was it done ? Oh , speak !

sections.label: Section 891
sections.translation: Of her own accord she set her hand to the deed .

sections.label: Section 892a
sections.translation: What are you saying ?

sections.label: Section 893
sections.translation: The firstborn , the firstborn of that newly arisen bride is

sections.label: Section 895
sections.translation: a great Erinys on this house !

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: ἄγαν γε · μᾶλλον δʼ , εἰ παροῦσα πλησία
sections.translation: Too true . And had you been close at hand and seen her do it , you would have pitied her even more .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἔτλη τις χεὶρ γυναικεία κτίσαι ;
sections.translation: And could a woman ’ s hand dare to carry out such deeds ?

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: δεινῶς γε · πεύσει δʼ , ὥστε μαρτυρεῖν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Yes , with fierce daring . You shall hear , and then you will bear me witness .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: ἐπεὶ γὰρ ἦλθε δωμάτων εἴσω μόνη
sections.translation: When she came alone into the house and saw her son preparing a deep litter in the court , with which to go back and meet his father , she hid herself where none would see . Falling before the altars , she moaned that

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: γένοιντʼ ἔρημοι , ʼκλαιε δʼ ὀργάνων ὅτου
sections.translation: they were left desolate , and whenever she touched any household thing that she , poor lady , had habitually used in the past , her tears would flow . If , as she roamed hither and thither through the house , she beheld the form of any well-loved servant , she wept in misery at the sight ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.translation:
crying aloud upon her own fate , and her childless existence thereafter .
But when she finished this , I saw her suddenly rush into the bedchamber of Heracles . I hid in the shadows to keep my observation secret and was watching over her

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: φρούρουν · ὁρῶ δὲ τὴν γυναῖκα δεμνίοις
sections.translation: when I saw her spread coverings on the bedstead of Heracles . When she had done this , she sprang up on them and sat in the middle of the bed . Then she broke out in warm streams of tears

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἔλεξεν · ὦ λέχη τε καὶ νυμφεῖʼ ἐμά ,
sections.translation: and said : “ Ah , my bridal bed and bridal chamber , farewell now and for ever , since you will never more receive me in these covers as his wife .” She says no more , but with a vehement hand she loosens her robe where the brooch of beaten gold

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: προύκειτο μαστῶν περονίς , ἐκ δʼ ἐλώπισεν
sections.translation: lies above her breasts and bares all her left side and arm . Then I run with all my strength , and warn her son of her intent . But even in the space between my going and our return ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.translation: we find that she has driven a two-edged sword up through her side to the heart . At that sight , her son gave a great cry . For he knew , poor boy , that in his anger he had driven her to that action . He had learned too late from the servants in the house

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ἄκουσα πρὸς τοῦ θηρὸς ἔρξειεν τάδε .
sections.translation: that she had acted without knowledge and at the will of the monster . Then and there the youth in his misery left no cry of grief unmade as he lamented over her , nor as he fell to kiss her lips ; he threw himself at her side , loudly moaning

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ὥς νιν ματαίως αἰτίᾳ βάλοι κακῇ ,
sections.translation:
that he had rashly attacked her with a false accusation and weeping that he must now live orphaned of both alike , of his father and of her .
Such are the fortunes of this house . Rash , indeed , is he who reckons on tomorrow , or perchance on days beyond it .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: μάταιός ἐστιν · οὐ γὰρ ἔσθʼ ἥ γʼ αὔριον ,
sections.translation: There is no tomorrow , until today is safely past . Exit the Nurse .

sections.label: Section 1.947
sections.translation: Which disaster shall I bewail first ? Which is more to be pitied ? In my grief I cannot decide .

sections.label: Section 1.950
sections.translation: One sorrow may be seen in the house ; for one we wait with apprehension . To have and to await are one and the same .

sections.label: Section 2.953
sections.translation: oh , that some strong breeze , a fair wind , might arise at our hearth

sections.label: Section 2.955
sections.translation: to bear me far from this land , so that I might not die of terror when I look but once

sections.label: Section 2.960
sections.translation: upon the mighty seed of Zeus ! For they say that he is approaching the house in torments from which there is no deliverance , a wonder of unutterable woe .

sections.label: Section 2.965
sections.translation: Ah , he was not far off , but close to us , he for whom I cried in advance , like the shrill nightingale ! Here is a foreign procession of strange men . And in what manner , then , do they bring him ? In sorrow , as for some loved one , they tread their mournful , noiseless tread . Ah , he is carried on in silence !

sections.label: Section 2.970
sections.translation: Should I think that he is dead , or just asleep ?

sections.label: Section 2.971
sections.translation: O my father ! O my misery ! What is to become of me ? What is my best plan ? Ah !

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: τί πάθω ; τί δὲ μήσομαι ; οἴμοι .
sections.translation: Hush , young man ! Do not rouse

sections.label: Section 975
sections.translation: the cruel pain that infuriates your father ! He lives , though on the very edge . Bite your tongue and hold it !

sections.label: Section 978
sections.translation: You must not awake the slumberer ! You must not rouse and revive

sections.label: Section 980
sections.translation: his fierce , recurrent infection , my son !

sections.label: Section 983
sections.translation: O Zeus , in what land am I ? Among what people

sections.label: Section 985
sections.translation: do I lie , tortured with unending agonies ? O me , what pain ! Oh , that accursed pest gnaws me once more !

sections.label: Section 989
sections.translation: Did I not know how much better it was that you should keep silent , instead of scattering

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: τῷδʼ ἀπὸ κρατὸς βλεφάρων θʼ ὕπνον ;
sections.translation: sleep from his brain and eyes ?

sections.label: Section 993
sections.translation: O Cenaean rock , foundation of my altars , what cruel reward

sections.label: Section 995
sections.translation: you have earned me for those fair offerings , O Zeus ! Ah , in what ruin you have deposited me , in what ruin ! Would that I had never looked on you to my sorrow with my eyes , never come face to face with this blooming madness , which no spell can soothe !

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: τίς γὰρ ἀοιδός , τίς ὁ χειροτέχνης
sections.translation: Where is the enchanter , where the practiced healer , save Zeus alone , who will charm this catastrophe away ? I would be amazed if I saw him from even a great distance !

sections.label: Section 1.1004
sections.translation: Ah !

sections.label: Section 1.1005
sections.original: ἐᾶτέ μʼ , ἐᾶτέ με δύσμορον ὕστατον ,
sections.translation: Leave me ! Leave me in my misfortune ! Leave me to sleep my last . Where , where do you touch me ? Where do you lay me ? You will kill me , you will kill me ! If there was any pain that slumbered , you have aroused it !

sections.label: Section 2.1010
sections.original: ἧπταί μου , τοτοτοῖ , ἥδʼ αὖθʼ ἕρπει . πόθεν ἔστʼ , ὦ
sections.translation: It has seized me ,— oh , the pest comes again !— What is your homeland , you men most ungrateful of all the Greeks ? I wore out my wretched days in ridding the Greeks of pests both on the deep and throughout every forest . And now , when I am afflicted , will no man turn merciful fire or sword on me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1015
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Will no one come and strike the head from this accursed body with one fierce stroke ? Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 1.1017
sections.original: ὦ παῖ τοῦδʼ ἀνδρός , τοὔργον τόδε μεῖζον ἀνήκει
sections.translation: You are the man ’ s son , and this task exceeds my strength . Help him , for the strength to save him is at your command ,

sections.label: Section 3.1024
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , ποῦ ποτʼ εἶ ; τᾷδέ με τᾷδέ με
sections.translation: O my son , where are you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1025
sections.original: πρόσλαβε κουφίσας . ἒ ἔ , ἰὼ δαῖμον .
sections.translation: Raise me , take hold of me here , here ! Oh , oh , my god ! Again , again the cruel pest leaps , leaps up to rend me , the

sections.label: Section 2.1030
sections.translation: wild , uncombatable plague ! O Pallas , Pallas , it tortures me again ! oh , please , my son , pity your sire ! Draw a sword — you will not be blamed for it — strike me beneath my collarbone .

sections.label: Section 2.1035
sections.original: παῖσον ἐμᾶς ὑπὸ κλῇδος · ἀκοῦ δʼ ἄχος , ᾧ μʼ ἐχόλωσεν
sections.translation: Heal this pain with which your godless mother has enraged me ! So may I see her fall to ruin , exactly , just exactly , as she has destroyed me !

sections.label: Section 2.1040
sections.original: αὔτως , ὧδʼ αὔτως ὥς μʼ ὤλεσεν . ὦ γλυκὺς Ἅιδας ,
sections.translation: Sweet Hades , brother of Zeus , give me sleep , give me sleep . Kill me in my misery by a swift-flying doom !

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.translation: I shudder , friends , to hear these sorrows
